title: RENAEST crash-record lifecycle and layout/completeness/duplicate rules
statement: RENAEST transactional endpoints enforce the crash-record lifecycle (RECEBIDO → EM_ANALISE → {CONSOLIDADO|REJEITADO}) and its business rules. Rules RENAEST.E001–E005 are enforced: an unsupported `versaoLeiaute` yields 400 RENAEST.CRASH.INVALID_LAYOUT (E001); a COM_VITIMA_* gravidade with no `vitimas`, or a missing `local`, yields 402 RENAEST.CRASH.INCOMPLETE_DATA (E002/E003); a same-tuple (uf, codigoMunicipio, dataHoraSinistro, orgaoResponsavel) resubmit without an Idempotency-Key yields 402 RENAEST.CRASH.DUPLICATED (E004); a complement/correction on a terminal record yields 402 RENAEST.CRASH.CORRECTION_NOT_ALLOWED (E005); an unknown idSinistro/protocolo yields 404 RENAEST.CRASH.NOT_FOUND. Writes are idempotent and audited per INV-IDEMP-001.
rationale: RENAEST is a national base that ingests crash/sinister records from state DETRANs and other agencies. The mock must behave like the national ingestion so pec/teat can exercise crash-submission, protocol-consultation and correction flows deterministically, including the rejection and duplicate branches, without touching real data.
